entries.title: API Replay Inspector
entries.notes: Uses service worker interception to capture network requests and replay them with modifications. One engineer can build the interception layer in a week. Browser DevTools only show raw requests—nothing lets you mutate and replay at scale.
entries.pain: Debugging API issues requires manually recreating requests, which is tedious and error-prone.

entries.title: LinkedIn Signal Tracker
entries.notes: Extracts LinkedIn profile data and enriches with recent activity signals. Browser extension handles scraping while a lightweight backend does enrichment. Solo SDRs need this more than enterprise sales tools.
entries.pain: Sales teams waste hours researching prospects before outreach, often missing key context.

entries.title: Tab Terrain
entries.notes: Groups tabs spatially by project and lets you navigate via keyboard. Built with tab group APIs plus a spatial canvas renderer. Offline-first, no backend needed for core feature.
entries.pain: Browser tabs accumulate faster than they can be organized, creating visual clutter and lost context.

entries.title: AI Spend Meter
entries.notes: Intercepts AI API calls and aggregates costs by domain with a live dashboard. Uses web request blocking to count tokens. Gap exists between provider dashboards and team-level tracking.
entries.pain: Teams overspend on AI APIs because there's no per-site visibility into usage and cost.

entries.title: Canvas Noise Injector
entries.notes: Injects randomized noise into canvas API calls while preserving normal rendering. More surgical than blocking all canvas access. Users increasingly aware of fingerprinting but lack lightweight tools.
entries.pain: Websites fingerprint users via canvas signatures without consent, tracking across the web.

entries.title: Env Drift Watcher
entries.notes: CLI companion polls env vars while extension shows diff alerts in browser toolbar. Small integration surface, high value for solo devs. No existing tool bridges local envs to live debugging.
entries.pain: Production bugs often stem from unnoticed differences between local and deployed environment variables.

entries.title: GraphQL Scope Lens
entries.notes: Pulls schema via introspection and renders query as interactive dependency graph. Uses D3 for visualization. Backend and frontend devs both struggle with GraphQL complexity daily.
entries.pain: Understanding nested GraphQL resolvers requires mental tracing through documentation and queries.

entries.title: Meeting Context Prep
entries.notes: Before calls, surfaces relevant CRM history and email threads in a side panel. Integrates with Google Calendar. Smaller play than full CRM but solves the immediate pain point.
entries.pain: Sales calls lack context from past emails and CRM notes, leading to disconnected conversations.

entries.title: Email Tone DNA
entries.notes: Analyzes reply patterns from email threads to suggest tonality adjustments. Novel angle on personalization beyond just inserting name/company. Tip jar model works well for solo sellers.
entries.pain: Cold emails feel generic when not tailored to a prospect's communication style and reply patterns.

entries.title: Page Highlight Sync
entries.notes: One-click capture to Readwise, Notion, or Obsidian with smart tagging. Uses readability extraction API for clean text. Bridges browser reading to personal knowledge systems.
entries.pain: Highlights from web articles don't sync to reading apps without manual export and reformatting.
